concepts.name: Tree Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in a tree data structure exactly once.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Tree Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a tree data structure, containing data and references to children.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a tree, with no parent.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.short_definition: A node directly connected to another node (parent) in a hierarchical structure.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has one or more child nodes.

concepts.name: Leaf Node
concepts.short_definition: A node with no children.

concepts.name: Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left child and right child.

concepts.name: Binary Search Tree (BST)
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in the left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in the right subtree are greater.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Call Stack
concepts.short_definition: The stack used by a program to keep track of active function calls.

concepts.name: Pre-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: DFS traversal: Visit the current node, then traverse the left subtree, then traverse the right subtree.

concepts.name: In-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: DFS traversal: Traverse the left subtree, then visit the current node, then traverse the right subtree.

concepts.name: Post-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: DFS traversal: Traverse the left subtree, then traverse the right subtree, then visit the current node.

concepts.name: Recursive Pre-order
concepts.short_definition: Implementing pre-order traversal using recursive function calls.

concepts.name: Iterative Pre-order
concepts.short_definition: Implementing pre-order traversal using an explicit stack.

concepts.name: Recursive In-order
concepts.short_definition: Implementing in-order traversal using recursive function calls.

concepts.name: Iterative In-order
concepts.short_definition: Implementing in-order traversal using an explicit stack.

concepts.name: Recursive Post-order
concepts.short_definition: Implementing post-order traversal using recursive function calls.

concepts.name: Iterative Post-order
concepts.short_definition: Implementing post-order traversal using an explicit stack.

concepts.name: Tree Reconstruction
concepts.short_definition: Constructing a tree from traversal sequences.

concepts.name: Tree Height
concepts.short_definition: The length of the longest path from the root to a leaf node.

concepts.name: Tree Width
concepts.short_definition: The maximum number of nodes at any level of the tree.

concepts.name: Tree Serialization
concepts.short_definition: Converting a tree into a format that can be stored or transmitted.

concepts.name: Tree Deserialization
concepts.short_definition: Reconstructing a tree from its serialized representation.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Ways to represent a graph, such as adjacency list or adjacency matrix.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each node has a list of its adjacent nodes.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where a matrix indicates the presence of edges between nodes.

concepts.name: Tree as a Graph
concepts.short_definition: Understanding a tree as a specific type of graph.

concepts.name: DFS on General Graphs
concepts.short_definition: Applying Depth-First Search to graphs that may contain cycles.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection to keep track of nodes that have already been visited during traversal.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains any cycles.

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.short_definition: A linear ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge uv, vertex u comes before v in the ordering.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Finding the maximal connected subgraphs within an undirected graph.

concepts.name: Backtracking
concepts.short_definition: A general algorithmic technique for finding all (or some) solutions to computational problems, notably constraint satisfaction problems, that incrementally builds candidates to the solutions, and abandons a candidate ('backtracks') as soon as it determines that the candidate cannot possibly be completed to a valid solution.

concepts.name: Tree Properties
concepts.short_definition: Fundamental attributes and rules that define a tree data structure.

concepts.name: Tree Algorithms
concepts.short_definition: Common algorithms and operations performed on trees.

concepts.name: Tree Applications
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and problems where trees are effectively used.
